ground_truth.title: Cheapest Website Builders: Get Online for Under $15
ground_truth.author: Emma Ryan
ground_truth.main_content:
Our March 2025 website builder testing found Hostinger to be the cheapest website builder, with plans starting from just $2.99 per month. Using a budget-friendly builder significantly reduces overhead costs, but you don’t have to sacrifice quality for price.

In this guide, I’ll walk you through each of our top-rated cheap website builders and share my recommendations, based on my own hands-on testing and more than 300 hours of research by our expert team.

The Cheapest Website Builders of 2025
1. Hostinger ($2.99) – Best site speed
2. Shopify ($5 Shopify Starter) – Best for online stores
3. Pixpa ($4.80) – Best for portfolios
4. GoDaddy ($10.99) – Best for beginners
5. Squarespace ($14.40 with code WBE10) – Best for creatives

Key Takeaways🔍
- When paying for a website builder subscription in bulk, you’ll often receive a heavily discounted introductory offer.
- Hostinger has the cheapest plans with the most long-term savings, but you’ll have to pay for two years upfront to make the most of this.
- Use code “WBE10” to save 10% on Squarespace’s plans.
- You can get Shopify’s introductory offer on a pay-as-you-go plan, but be aware that the offer only lasts three months.
- To avoid being caught off guard by price hikes, be aware of the length of any introductory offers and the renewal fees before subscribing.

Cheapest Website Builders: At a Glance
Aside from price, there’s more to factor in to make sure you choose the right website building platform for your needs. Here’s a quick overview of our top cheap website builders:

Why you can trust us 🤝
At Website Builder Expert, our unbiased reviews, guides, and comparisons are backed up by over 300 hours of data collection and research. I’ve also tested every builder in this article, so I can bring you independent recommendations for the top website builders for creating your own site.
You can read more about our unique research methodology here.

1. Hostinger: $2.99 per month
Hostinger is the most affordable website builder on this list, with plans starting from $2.99 per month. It’s an ideal option for beginners and small business owners who want to make use of its user-friendly drag-and-drop editor and ever-growing suite of AI tools.
However, while its AI tools might be advanced, it lacks other basic functionality. For example, there’s no app store or email marketing features – if these are essential for your site, Squarespace and Shopify will give you access to better tools.

How Much Does Hostinger Cost?
Hostinger’s pricing is split between two website builder plans: Premium and Business.
Hostinger runs limited student discounts through its partner platform, Student Beans. Plus, users who sign up for a 48-month term of either plan can take advantage of Hostinger’s introductory deal. This gives you three months free, a year’s free domain, and a heavily discounted monthly price:
Premium Website Builder: $2.99 per month
Business Website Builder: $3.99 per month
After your first two years are up, you’ll be charged the standard monthly price:
$10.99 per month
$13.99 per month
Hostinger doesn’t charge any transaction fees on its ecommerce plan, making it the most affordable entry into selling online.

Are Hostinger’s Plans Affordable Long Term?
Both of Hostinger’s plans are very affordable, and with only a $1 price difference between them, it’s risk-free to upgrade or downgrade as you see fit.
To unlock Hostinger’s full suite of AI tools and ecommerce features, you’ll need the Business plan. Still, the majority of core website features, such as a free domain name and access to the AI website builder, come with the cheaper plan. If you’re unsure which to choose, Hostinger offers a seven-day free trial, as well as a 30-day money-back guarantee.

Is Hostinger Good Value for Money?
Hostinger is the cheapest website builder on this list, which makes it an affordable option for budget conscious beginners looking to test the waters. Still, our testing indicates that GoDaddy and Squarespace offer a greater quality and variety of features compared to Hostinger, providing better value despite the higher cost.
While Hostinger has continued to hone its suite of AI tools this year, including the AI Product Information Generator and Kodee, a built-in AI assistant to help you set up and manage your website, it’s still lacking the fundamental – and arguably more important – features that make up a well-rounded builder. For instance, you won’t be able to carry out email and social media marketing.

My verdict: Is Hostinger worth the money?
I really enjoyed setting up a website with Hostinger because of its AI-powered builder and tools. This made the whole experience easy and quick – in my opinion, it rivals Squarespace for ease of use. However, because of its more limited suite of features and lack of key functionality (such as email marketing), Hostinger is best for very small businesses and personal, informational sites. At such an affordable price point, it’s definitely worth the money.

2. Shopify: $5 per month Shopify Starter plan
Shopify is our top-rated ecommerce website builder, offering an affordable $5 per month Starter plan – though its actual website builder plans are much more expensive. Shopify’s Starter plan enables you to sell on social media (such as Instagram and WhatsApp), and includes basic inventory management tools, analytics to monitor selling, and a custom Linkpop page to drive customers to products. However, it doesn’t include the ability to build an ecommerce website.

How Much Does Shopify Cost?
Shopify’s pricing is more expensive than other builders on this list, particularly Hostinger. Besides the limited $5 per month Shopify Starter plan, there are three core premium plans:
Basic: $29 per month
Grow: $79 per month
Advanced: $299 per month
Shopify doesn’t have a long term discount for new starters like other options on this list, but it has a three-day free trial and you can get your first three months for just $1.
Ecommerce sellers will be charged a 2% transaction fee on Shopify’s Basic plan, which will lower to 0.6% as you upgrade. Alternatively, using Shopify’s native gateway, Shopify Payments, will remove this fee all together and save you money.

Are Shopify’s Plans Affordable Long Term?
There’s a steep price hike between Shopify’s Starter and Core plans, which can be hard to justify as your business grows. For instance, upgrading from the Starter to the Basic plan will increase your monthly spend by $24. On top of that, Shopify’s introductory offer only offers three months of savings, unlike other builders on this list that give you a discount for a whole year.
With basic analytics and the ability to sell unlimited products, Shopify Starter is a great choice for casual sellers building up a customer base without their own online store. However, to access advanced selling features—such as discount codes and greater customization options to showcase your brand—you’ll need to pay for one of Shopify’s core premium plans.

Is Shopify Good Value for Money?
Shopify ranks lowest for value among these five builders due to its higher price point relative to standard website building features. However, if your business requires ecommerce functionality and has the budget, Shopify’s powerful sales features are worth the cost.
Given the high costs, Shopify does give you access to powerful ecommerce features from its cheapest plan. The same can’t be said for other website builders, like GoDaddy or Squarespace.

My verdict: Is Shopify's Starter plan worth the money?
If you’re simply selling on the side and want to focus on socials, then the Shopify Starter plan is a great deal. However, I really recommend upgrading to one of Shopify’s core premium plans to build a polished website for your online store. Yes, these are more expensive than other builders on this list, but you unlock a lot of impressive sales features to support your ecommerce journey. If you have the budget to spare and want to sell online, a dedicated ecommerce builder like Shopify is well worth it.

Read our Q&A with a real Shopify business:
Q&A
1. What did you initially think about Shopify's pricing, and how did you decide that it was a good fit for your business?
Initially, Shopify’s pricing structure felt very reasonable, especially considering the features offered. We started with the Basic Shopify plan, which was perfect for launching our online leatherwear store. We could showcase as many products as we wanted, track our inventory across multiple locations, and still have good checkout capacity on big sales events.
As our sales grew, upgrading to the Advanced plan provided the additional features we needed to manage inventory and discounts more effectively. The feature of localized selling with custom markets with the advanced plan was a huge plus, allowing us to scale our business growth.

2. Which Shopify features do you think are great value for money?
Several Shopify features have been immensely helpful for us. The built-in abandoned cart recovery has been fantastic for capturing missed sales, and the discount code functionality allows us to run targeted promotions that resonate with our customer base. We also find the ease of adding high-quality product photos and descriptions to be invaluable. It allows us to showcase the rich textures and craftsmanship of our leather goods beautifully.
Customizing our selected Shopify theme, Turbo!, to align with our store’s unique style and objectives proved to be an invaluable investment. The ability to tailor every aspect to our brand’s vision not only justified the cost but also enhanced the overall shopping journey for our customers, ensuring a seamless and delightful experience from start to finish.

3. Do you have any budgeting tips when using Shopify?
Here are a couple of budgeting tips we’ve learned along the way:
We embraced a “start small, grow big” mentality. We began with a modest budget, focusing on core functionalities rather than fancy extras. This allowed us to test the waters and refine our approach before scaling up our spending alongside our business growth.
Choosing the right Shopify plan was crucial. We didn’t get swept up in the most expensive option. Instead, we carefully evaluated features and pricing to find a plan that perfectly fit our needs and growth projections. This ensured we maximized the value we received from Shopify’s platform.
Shopify offers a treasure trove of free apps and tools. We took full advantage of them to enhance our store’s functionality without breaking the bank. We only invested in premium apps when they addressed a specific need that couldn’t be met with a free alternative.
Keeping a watchful eye on inventory costs has been essential. We’ve implemented strategies to avoid both overstocking and understocking. This helps us minimize carrying costs and ensure we always have the right products in stock to meet customer demand.

3. Pixpa: $4.80 per month
Starting at $4.80 per month, Pixpa is an ideal cheap website builder for visual and creative businesses, particularly if you’re looking to build a portfolio or artist website. Its cheapest plan gives you access to basic features, including SEO and social media integration, but its primary selling point is that its includes online selling capabilities. For context, GoDaddy’s equivalent ecommerce plan will set you back $23.99 per month.
That said, Pixpa’s sales features pale in comparison to other platforms, such as Shopify. Its analytics and accounting tools are limited, so you’ll struggle to scale your online store as successfully as you would with another option. It’s for this reason that I recommend Pixpa for portfolios and simple sites.

How Much Does Pixpa Cost?
Pixpa offers four premium plans to choose between: Basic, Creator, Professional, and Advanced. When billed annually, you’ll receive a 40% discount on the plans. If you’re a student or educator in a creative field, you’ll be able to get a 55% price cut. Annual prices are as follows:
Basic: $4.80 per month
Creator: $9 per month
Professional: $12 per month
Advanced: $15 per month
After the first year, plans will return to the regular price:
$8 per month
$15 per month
$20 per month
$25 per month
Even on its cheapest plan, Pixpa doesn’t charge any transaction fees, making it an attractive option for new sellers. Before you decide what plan to pick, you can test out Pixpa’s editor and tools with its 15-day free trial.

Are Pixpa’s Plans Affordable Long Term?
Our testing found Pixpa’s price range to be very competitive in comparison to other builders’. It’s fairly similar in price to GoDaddy, but Pixpa’s focus is on creatives and portfolio sites especially.
The cheapest Basic plan is pretty limited (it only allows up to 10 pages and 200 gallery images), so I recommend starting with at least the Creator plan to unlock extra features, including unlimited pages/images and blogging functionality. There’s around a $5 difference between each plan, which lightens the strain of upgrading as your portfolio begins to expand.

Is Pixpa Good Value for Money?
Pixpa’s Basic plan does offer a lot of the standard features you’d expect from a website builder, such as SEO, which is good considering the low price point. But you’ll need to upgrade to at least the Creator plan ($15/mo) for unlimited website pages and images, blogging functionality, a free domain name, advanced SEO like 301 redirects, and additional selling tools, such as coupons. For advanced features like abandoned cart recovery, you’ll have to sign up for the Advanced plan, which costs $25 per month (billed annually).
To get the most out of your money, I recommend choosing the Professional plan since this unlocks almost every feature.

My Verdict: Is Pixpa worth the money?
With pretty cheap premium plans, Pixpa won’t break the bank. However, I’d argue it’s only worth spending your money on Pixpa’s website builder if you want to launch a specialist, creative website. Its features and designs target portfolios in particular, so you won’t have the flexibility provided by other builders which cater to a wider range of industry types

4. GoDaddy: $10.99 per month
GoDaddy is an incredibly cheap website builder, with premium plans starting at $10.99 per month, as well as a free-forever plan. GoDaddy’s fast and intuitive onboarding process makes it the best option for beginners looking to get online quickly – our first-hand testing found it could generate a website in less than 30 seconds.
Its editor is super straightforward to understand, and while GoDaddy lacks many stylish designs or creative capabilities, the simple elements make GoDaddy a good option for content-heavy sites.

How Much Does GoDaddy Cost?
You can get started with GoDaddy’s free-forever plan, but it comes with ads and limited storage. I recommend opting for one of GoDaddy’s three premium plans instead: Basic, Premium, and Commerce.
When you commit to a year’s subscription, an introductory discount of between 40% and 50% will be applied, depending on the GoDaddy plan you choose. Plus, GoDaddy has reinstated its 12-month free trial Microsoft professional email, which would set you back $95.
The introductory prices (when billed annually) are as follows:
Basic: $10.99 per month
Premium: $16.99 per month
Commerce: $23.99 per month
After your first year is up, the plans increase to their original pricing:
$16.99 per month
$29.99 per month
$34.99 per month
Ecommerce sellers should also expect to pay GoDaddy a flat rate transaction fee of 2.7% + $0.30 per sale.

Are GoDaddy’s Plans Affordable Long Term?
GoDaddy’s pricing is very affordable overall, but you’ll need the $23.99 per month (billed annually) Commerce plan to unlock ecommerce functionality. If you’re just after a basic site and don’t need to sell products, you’ll be fine with the Basic or Premium plan.

Is GoDaddy Good Value For Money?
GoDaddy’s plans are very affordable, though it recently increased its prices slightly across all plans. For example, the Basic plan used to cost $9.99 but it now costs $10.99. Still, GoDaddy is cheap and it’s the best value for money builder on this list! You get a decent amount of features for the price point, and its ecommerce plan is a cheaper alternative to builders like Wix or Squarespace.
Plus, GoDaddy offers a free plan which you can stay on for as long as you’d like. However, I’d recommend upgrading to remove ads and unlock additional features, such as ecommerce.

My verdict: Is GoDaddy worth the money?
GoDaddy tops our research when it comes to value for money, so it’s absolutely worth the money. Its plans are incredibly cheap, but you can find your feet with its editor and tools through the free plan first. And, even if you want to sell online on the Commerce plan, the $23.99 per month price tag is still cheaper than other ecommerce builders, like Shopify.

5. Squarespace: $14.40 per month with our code “WBE10”
Squarespace is the most expensive website builder on this list, at $14.40 per month with our code “WBE10.” However, Squarespace was scored as the easiest platform to use in our testing due to its highly intuitive editor. Squarespace is the best choice if you’re a creative in need of affordable website design, thanks to its stunning templates and elements.

How Much Does Squarespace Cost?
Squarespace’s pricing consists of four plans: Basic, Core, Plus, and Advanced.
Each annual plan provides a free custom domain and students are eligible for a 50% discount. And when you use the code “WBE10” at checkout, you’ll save an extra 10%, which takes each plan to the following price:
Basic: $14.40 per month
Core: $20.70 per month
Plus: $35.10 per month
Advanced: $89.10 per month
After your first year with Squarespace, your plan will renew at the standard annual price:
$16 per month
$23 per month
$39 per month
$99 per month
Squarespace’s cheapest plan charges a 2% transaction fee, which is waived upon upgrading to the Core plan or above. You can get started with Squarespace’s 14-day free trial to see whether it’s right for you.

Are Squarespace’s Plans Affordable Long Term?
After Squarespace’s pricing change this year, you can start selling on its cheapest $14.40 per month plan, meaning there’s less financial pressure to upgrade quickly. However, there is now a bigger jump between plan prices, with the Advanced plan way over double the price of the Plus plan. This makes it much harder to scale your business smoothly.
Still, once you upgrade to the Core plan, Squarespace softens the blow by removing transaction fees entirely and providing additional sales features.
